ops.body:
## Decision (2026-07-14)

Three changes shipped together after the implementing-subagent latency investigation:

1. **Fleet effort pin max → xhigh** (`plugin/guides/json/settings-base.json`). 39/41 repos pinned `effortLevel: max`, overriding the documented "xhigh default, max on escalation" convention; at max, thinking was ~half of every implementing subagent's output.
2. **Models table v5** (`plugin/guides/md/claude-rules.md`): gpt-5.6-sol xhigh becomes the **default implementation lane for bounded, decision-light work** (the plan/work order/repeated pattern already made the decisions; execution remains), including repetitive bounded sweeps as codex-wrapper fan-outs. Opus keeps ambiguous, exploratory, long-horizon, or **decision-dense** work — mid-flight judgment calls pile up even when scope is bounded. Decompose-first rule: implementation that splits into bounded decision-light units fans out to sol rather than running as one long opus agent. Nested spawns follow the table (a subagent/teammate routes its implementers exactly as the orchestrator would). Sol's two lane gates (user-supplied nuance): judgment degrades as mid-task decisions pile up, and it matches surrounding code conventions less faithfully — backstopped by finder/refuter review + capt-hook guards, so lean sol harder where capt-hook coverage is live.
3. **Sweeps are workflows, not teams**: teammate subagents are foreground-only (code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-teams#limitations), so bounded N-unit sweeps run as Workflow fan-outs; teams stay a handful of long-running peers (docs/en/workflows#when-to-use-a-workflow).

## Measured basis (local, 2026-07-14)

Live 1h43m p3-tests teammate (cc-transcript, opus-4.8, v14 test-suite reconstruction) + 25 subagent transcripts + 78 codex rollouts:
- ~50% of teammate wall-clock was model generation (73–86% on non-delegating implementers); 46–55% of generated chars were thinking tokens (15–30s/turn) under the fleet `max` pin.
- ~33% waiting on 11 nested opus module-conversion agents (522s/433s single waits) after `run_in_background: true` errored — the documented teams limitation.
- ~14% real tool work; ~0% API errors.
- The v4 sol implementation lane had **zero adoption**: 0 `apply_patch` across two days of codex sessions; all 11 nested spawns were textbook sol-lane bounded conversions and every one went to opus.

## Online basis (Artificial Analysis, 2026-07-14)

sol xhigh vs opus-4.8 max: 66 vs 54 tok/s output; TTFT 35.8s vs 40.4s; Intelligence Index v4.1 58 vs 56; Coding Agent Index sol 80 (leads all) at ~10% cheaper per task; opus-4.8 ~2× tier-median output tokens per task (matches the measured thinking share). Sol's speed case is **token efficiency, not a wall-clock multiplier** — no independent head-to-head exists (the "half the time" claim is OpenAI's, vs Fable, at max effort); never cite a multiplier. Sol stays pinned xhigh: at max its TTFT inverts to 131s vs opus's 40s, so max is not a sol escalation rung — escalation stays fable.
Sources: artificialanalysis.ai/articles/gpt-5-6-has-landed and artificialanalysis.ai/models/comparisons/gpt-5-6-sol-xhigh-vs-claude-opus-4-8

## Shipped

- cc-skills `55374f2a` (pack: claude-rules v5, parallelize.md, settings-base xhigh, base-conventions retell, repo-bootstrap 0.53.0) + `069ac48d` (render), CI green.
- captain-hook `e49b6a8b`: general pack **0.22.0** nudge messages + classifier rubrics reworded to the decision-density split; released **v9.14.0** (PyPI publish green). Release gotcha: creating the GitHub release manually collided with the pipeline's own `github-release` job (it creates the release + attaches dist) — in this repo cut releases by pushing the tag only; assets were reconciled by hand this time.
- Fleet render sweep: Workflow fan-out over 37 consumer repos (all `~/Code/*` with `.claude/fragments/cc-guides.lock`, minus cc-skills), on top of the daily guides.yml cron which had already run at 10:56Z — before the pack landed.

## Correction (same day)

The user's verdict on the render sweep above: wasted effort — never repeat it. A pack change that lands after the daily render window simply rides the next day's cron (fleet-wide within 24h). The sweep enablers were removed and the daily-pull invariant audited 43/43 green the same day: see note 878bdd49 and memory `never-sweep-guides-fleet`. Do not copy the sweep pattern from this note.
